rxn_class_summary: Electrosynthesis of substituted cyclopropanecarboxylic acid esters via cathodic reduction of methyl trichloroacetate (MTCA) or methyl dichloroacetate (MDCA) in the presence of acrylonitrile (AcN), yielding methyl 1-chloro-2-cyanocyclopropanecarboxylate.
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction: two-electron dissociative reduction of MTCA (C–Cl cleavage) at Hg cathode generates a stabilized dichlorocarbanion (CCl2C(O)OMe-); alternatively, cathodic reduction of residual water generates OH-/Et4NOH which deprotonates MDCA to the same anion (hidden indirect deprotonation, pathway II).

key_intermediates.name: Michael adduct anion from carbanion + acrylonitrile (CCl2(CO2Me)-CH2-CH(-)-CN)
tetraethylammonium hydroxide (Et4NOH) as in situ base
linear adduct 'm' EtO2C-CCl2-CH2-CHCl-CN (claimed in ref [6], not observed here)

electron_flow: At the Hg cathode, MTCA accepts 2e- with loss of Cl- to give the CCl2CO2Me carbanion, which undergoes Michael addition to acrylonitrile; the resulting carbanion displaces an intramolecular Cl- to close the cyclopropane ring. For MDCA on steel/Hg, cathodic reduction of H2O gives OH- (then Et4NOH via Et4NCl), which deprotonates MDCA to the same CCl2CO2Me anion that then follows the Michael/ring-closure sequence.
rate_determining_step: Initial C–Cl dissociative electron transfer (for MTCA) or cathodic water discharge / deprotonation of MDCA (for MDCA pathway II)

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of MDCA on Pt in Et4NClO4/MeCN
probe_reagents.observation: Reverse-scan anodic peak for Cl- oxidation observed, indicating that on low-H-overpotential cathodes MDCA reduction still proceeds with C–Cl cleavage rather than C–H deprotonation.

probe_reagents.name: solvent variation (DMF vs MeCN) and electrolyte (Et4NCl vs LiCl)
probe_reagents.observation: Substitution of LiCl for Et4NCl lowers current efficiency; MeCN on steel recovers cyclopropane yield comparable to Hg/DMF, supporting Et4NOH as in situ base (pathway II).

probe_reagents.name: cathode material variation (Hg vs steel)
probe_reagents.observation: On steel cathode no cyclopropane 'b' (which would indicate cathodic dechlorination of MDCA) is detected, supporting the deprotonation pathway II for MDCA.

probe_reagents.name: 1H NMR and GC-MS product analysis
probe_reagents.observation: Cyclopropane 'a' identified (M+ = 125); no linear product 'm' detected, contradicting the halogenation mechanism proposed in ref [6].

notes: Two pathways are proposed for cyclopropane 'a' formation: Pathway I (favored for MTCA) — direct 2e cathodic dissociative reduction of MTCA to the CCl2CO2Me carbanion, Michael addition to acrylonitrile, intramolecular SN2 ring closure. Pathway II (favored for MDCA, especially on steel) — 'hidden indirect deprotonation' where cathodic water reduction generates OH-, which together with Et4NCl yields Et4NOH that deprotonates MDCA to the same carbanion. The earlier mechanism 'm' from ref [6], invoking MTCA as a halogenating agent to give a linear chloronitrile, is rejected because product 'm' was not detected by 1H NMR or GC-MS in this work.
